name: London
description: London is one of the world's great cities — free world-class museums, extraordinary theatre, a dining scene that rivals Paris, and neighbourhoods so distinct they each feel like separate cities.

name: Edinburgh
description: Edinburgh is one of the world's great capital cities — a dramatic castle on a volcanic rock, a medieval Old Town, a Georgian New Town, and some of the world's best whisky bars.

name: Glasgow
description: Glasgow is Scotland's most vibrant city — a brilliant music scene, world-class art galleries, outstanding food, and a warmth of character that makes it one of Europe's most welcoming cities.

name: Scottish Highlands
description: The Scottish Highlands offer some of Europe's most dramatic and unspoiled landscapes — lochs, glens, ancient castles, and a solitude that's increasingly hard to find anywhere on the continent.

name: Dublin
description: Dublin's pub culture is the stuff of legend — live traditional music, extraordinary conversation, and a warmth that makes strangers feel like old friends within minutes. Compact, walkable, and endlessly entertaining.

name: Galway
description: Ireland's most characterful city is a gateway to the wild Atlantic Way — a bohemian, music-filled town with a brilliant food scene and the dramatic Connemara landscape on its doorstep.

name: Ring of Kerry
description: One of Europe's most scenic drives — 179km of Atlantic coastline, mountains, lakes, and ancient ruins through County Kerry. Spectacular at any time of year.

name: Copenhagen
description: Copenhagen consistently ranks among the world's most liveable cities. The food scene is extraordinary, the design culture is inspiring, and the waterfront Nyhavn district is one of Europe's most photogenic.

name: Reykjavik
description: Iceland's tiny capital is the gateway to some of the world's most dramatic landscapes — northern lights, geysers, waterfalls, and black sand beaches all within a few hours' drive.

name: Stockholm
description: Stockholm is spread across 14 islands — a strikingly beautiful capital with outstanding museums, a vibrant food scene, and Gamla Stan, one of Europe's best-preserved medieval old towns.

name: Oslo
description: Oslo is compact, clean, and quietly extraordinary — the Viking Ship Museum, the Munch Museum, and the stunning Oslofjord. The gateway to Norway's fjords.

name: Bergen
description: Bergen is the gateway to Norway's UNESCO-listed fjords — a charming harbor city of painted wooden houses, fish markets, and cable car rides to mountain viewpoints.

name: Helsinki
description: Helsinki is a design-forward, sea-facing capital with a distinctive Nordic-Russian character, outstanding food, genuine sauna culture, and island archipelagos just minutes from the city.

name: Faroe Islands
description: The Faroe Islands are 18 volcanic islands in the North Atlantic — dramatic sea cliffs, turf-roofed villages, and landscapes of such raw beauty they look almost computer-generated.

name: Lofoten Islands
description: Dramatic peaks rising from the Arctic Ocean, red fishing cabins on stilts, northern lights in winter, and midnight sun in summer — the Lofoten Islands are among the most spectacular places in Europe.

name: Amsterdam
description: Amsterdam's canal-ringed streets, world-class museums, and legendary café culture make it one of Europe's most liveable cities. Endlessly explorable by bike.

name: Bruges
description: Bruges is a medieval masterpiece — canals, Gothic architecture, chocolate shops, and a pace of life that feels centuries removed from the modern world.

name: Brussels
description: Brussels is more than its EU reputation — Art Nouveau architecture, extraordinary waffles and frites, some of the world's best beer, and the magnificent Grand Place.

name: Ghent
description: Ghent is Belgium's most overlooked city — a spectacular medieval center with three towers, one of Europe's greatest paintings, and a thriving craft beer and restaurant scene.

name: Zurich
description: Switzerland's largest city sits on a lake with Alps on the horizon — extraordinarily beautiful, impeccably clean, and a gateway to world-class skiing and hiking.

name: Geneva
description: Geneva sits at the tip of Lake Geneva beneath the Alps — the world's most international city has extraordinary watchmaking heritage, world-class restaurants, and easy access to both Swiss and French Alps.

name: Interlaken
description: Nestled between two lakes with the Jungfrau massif towering above, Interlaken is the adventure sports capital of the Alps — paragliding, skydiving, and world-class skiing.

name: Lucerne
description: Lucerne's medieval covered bridges, lake setting, and mountain backdrop make it one of Central Europe's most scenic towns.

name: Prague
description: Prague's fairy-tale old town is one of Europe's best-preserved historic city centers. Far more affordable than Western Europe with outstanding food, beer, and culture.

name: Budapest
description: Budapest is one of Europe's most dramatic capitals — stunning architecture split across the Danube, legendary thermal baths, ruin bars, and a nightlife scene that rivals Berlin.

name: Krakow
description: Krakow's remarkably intact medieval old town is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the city has a vibrant bar and restaurant scene. One of Europe's most affordable great cities.

name: Warsaw
description: Warsaw has reinvented itself as one of Europe's most dynamic cities — a meticulously rebuilt old town, a booming food and cocktail scene, and an extraordinary story of resilience.

name: Ljubljana
description: Slovenia's tiny capital is one of Europe's best-kept secrets — a car-free old town, a hilltop castle, and the stunning Lake Bled just 45 minutes away.

name: Lake Bled
description: A glacial lake with a fairy-tale island church and a clifftop castle — Lake Bled looks almost too beautiful to be real. Perfect as an overnight stay.

name: Tallinn
description: Tallinn's medieval old town is one of the best-preserved in Europe — cobblestone streets, ancient towers, and ramparts surrounding a beautifully walkable city center.

name: Riga
description: Riga has the largest collection of Art Nouveau architecture in the world, a beautiful medieval old town, and a surprisingly vibrant nightlife and food scene.

name: Vilnius
description: Vilnius has one of Eastern Europe's finest baroque old towns — a UNESCO site that feels authentically untouristed, with great coffee shops and a thriving arts scene.
